activation_status.reason: capsule directory is inside the live campaign's support root; reading it could race the running teacher capture, so it is reported, not opened

bench_spec_policy.law: gravity_bench_lab.BenchSpec; every candidate for one geometry shares one spec, so no comparison crosses differing specs
bench_spec_policy.statistics: min and median are the trustworthy readings on this contended box; p95 and max are contention and are carried, never averaged

refusal_sites.source: gravity_metal.py matvec: 'this kernel handles subspaces == 1 only'
gravity_metal.py matvec: 'rotated geometry is not wired into this kernel yet'
gravity_metal.py matvec: 'k > 256 exceeds the one-byte index this kernel uses'

production_v2.provenance: the shipped kernel; 1D grid, const uint row = gid, GRID_UNDERFILLED

refusal_sites.source: gravity_metal_lab_b.dfma_plan: 'the float4 inner loop needs D % 4 == 0'
gravity_metal_lab_b.TrackBDecoder.upload: 'index ... out of range for k'

decode_fma_2d.provenance: GLM52_TRACK_A_BENCHMARK.json: first configuration faster than dense fp16

refusal_sites.source: gravity_metal_lab_b.ll_plan: 'cbs=... needs ... B of threadgroup memory ... limit is ...'

shape_priors.measurement: reports/condense/breakthrough/GLM52_TRACK_A_BENCHMARK.json: median-of-medians by chunk-block count, gate 0.9913 -> 0.3602 -> 0.3291 -> 0.3158 ms at blocks 1/8/32/64; attention 2.2086 -> 0.5782 -> 0.4745 -> 0.4714. Saturation 32-64; blocks=1 (the production grid: one dispatch, no reduce tax) is the worst row at every geometry. blocks=1 is swept HERE because it is the only way to put a GPU clock on the production grid -- gravity_metal.matvec exposes no GPUEndTime.
reports/condense/breakthrough/GLM52_TRACK_A_BENCHMARK.json: best shapes were gate tpg256/blk64, down tpg64/blk64, attention tpg64/blk32.

reports/condense/breakthrough/GLM52_TRACK_B_BENCHMARK.json: the fp32 table caps cbs at 52 (4096 + 544*cbs <= 32768); half-precision T raises it to 99 and buys no time while costing 823x-1189x in parity, so it is not swept.
reports/condense/breakthrough/GLM52_TRACK_B_BENCHMARK.json: row4 (uchar4 load, 4 accumulators) is in every winning variant at both ends; tpg sets rows per thread.

closed_levers.measurement: reports/condense/breakthrough/GLM52_TRACK_A_BENCHMARK.json: b7/u8 time ratio median 0.995/1.018/0.997 at the three geometries; it removes 12.5% of the index stream, which is 0.4%-2.6% of executed bytes.
reports/condense/breakthrough/GLM52_TRACK_B_BENCHMARK.json: 15.84x slower on GPU time at gate, 5.39x at down.

reports/condense/breakthrough/GLM52_TRACK_B_BENCHMARK.json: 3.19x on GPU time with 16 real index streams on one real book, but 60 of 60 codebooks on one shard hash distinctly. Needs a full re-pack of 1.507 TB plus a new quality gate; quality cost UNMEASURED.
reports/condense/breakthrough/GLM52_BREAKTHROUGH_BASELINE.json: 1.18x gate/up, 1.58x down, entirely from removing command buffers; per-dispatch GPU time does not improve with batching.

criteria.source: mandate 2: a kernel that cannot consume the artifact is not a candidate regardless of speed. Evaluated by Kernel.accepts against the shard header.

gravity_metal.py's own tolerance, 2e-3. A 1e-6 gate would fail for the right reason and be misread: real codebooks are fp16 on disk so the kernel's fp16 cast is a no-op (reports/condense/breakthrough/GLM52_BREAKTHROUGH_BASELINE.json: codebook_fp16_roundtrip_lossless=true, max delta exactly 0.0).

what a caller pays, and the only metric all four implementations expose, so it is where production_v2 is judged. It ELIMINATES and never DECIDES: the measured 215.8 us command-buffer fixed cost is 70-90% of every candidate's wall at one matvec, so a wall gap of a few percent is host noise on a shared constant, not a grammar fact (reports/condense/breakthrough/GLM52_TRACK_B_BENCHMARK.json: 'a shape chosen there is chosen under the wrong regime'). The 20% band is that constant's share made explicit: 20% of a ~0.27 ms wall is 54 us, about a quarter of the constant.

GPUEndTime-GPUStartTime from the driver: the component the GRAMMAR controls. The 5% band is the campaign's standing rule on a contended box (reports/condense/breakthrough/GLM52_TRACK_B_BENCHMARK.json: verdicts require median AND min to clear +-5%). gravity_metal.matvec exposes no GPU clock, so production_v2 is dropped here with that reason recorded; its grid is measured under a clock as decode_fma_2d blocks=1.

the confirming statistic. Track B's law is that median AND min must both clear the band; when they disagree the candidates are inside noise and the decision falls through to the analytic criteria below.
mandate 2 bandwidth criterion. ANALYTIC upper bound from gravity_metal_lab_b.ll_cost / dfma_cost / gravity_metal.matvec_bytes.
mandate 2 arithmetic criterion. Same analytic ledgers.

mandate 2 resource criterion: threadgroup memory caps residency per core. reports/condense/breakthrough/GLM52_BREAKTHROUGH_BASELINE.json/diagnostic: 28,672 B of scratch capped a fused grid at ~1 TG/core and cost 4.8x.

hybrid_within_tensor.reason: mixing the two grammars WITHIN one matvec would need a kernel that builds the lookup table for some chunk blocks and decodes the rest per row in the same dispatch. No such kernel is compiled in this worktree. Both existing grammars already carry the 2D (row tile x chunk block) split, so the split is not the axis a within-tensor hybrid would add.
hybrid_within_tensor.what_does_exist: hybrid_mixed_grammar_batch -- one command buffer per layer in which each tensor is encoded with the grammar this matrix selected for its geometry. That is cross-tensor, is buildable from gravity_metal_lab_b.TrackBDecoder.run_batch today, and is MEASURED below.

artifact_compatibility_gate.finding: the gate is live and never fires on THESE artifacts: every safe shard is rung R0 (S=1, rotate=False, k=128, D=8, 7-bit indices) and every tensor has rows % 4 == 0, so all three grammars can decode everything. It fires in the unit tests, which is the only place a rotated / multi-subspace / k>256 / odd-rows artifact exists.

what_would_be_refused.rotated artifact: all three -- no kernel has a rotation path
what_would_be_refused.k > 256: all three -- the index buffer is uint8

shape_stability.model: two real tensors of one shape are an internal replicate of the selection
shape_stability.consequence: where a shape splits, the two grammars are interchangeable at that geometry and the byte ledger, not the clock, should pick.

real_skewed_gate_proj.note: the real hot end; measured as a geometry row above

real_near_uniform_down_proj.note: the real cold end; measured as a geometry row above

spread.construction: round-robin over all k codewords (cold extreme)
round-robin over all k codewords (cold extreme)

hybrid_mixed_grammar_batch.definition: one command buffer, each tensor encoded with the grammar this matrix selected for its geometry

whole_token_ledger.model: ANALYTIC byte model per kernel, multiplied by the ledger's per-token counts
whole_token_ledger.caveat: the executed model is an upper bound whose re-read terms the caches partly serve; Track A measured above the 736 GB/s roof under it at the attention geometry. True traffic lies between executed and unique.
